<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997413 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02092: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard

- page_id: `303997413`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997413/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:09.395+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

907302760

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

907302781

907302758

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

907302771

You can create several different sets of instances using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard. You can add each set to a different package and display it on a newly created diagram.

Depending on the size of your system, a very large number of instance specifications can be created using the wizard. That is why we strongly recommend selecting a new package wherein all created instance specifications will be stored.

##### Step #1. Selecting parts to be instantiated

In this step, you can see all the available instance specification values (parts) of your selected element. At this point, you can select which parts or properties to instantiate, create or select values for parts and properties, as well as add or remove parallel parts.

You can select/deselect the parts by clicking the check marks next to the parts or the options in the Selection area.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Parts are displayed if the multiplicity is greater than 1. The number of parts displayed depends on the multiplicity.

- If the multiplicity is a particular value, for example 2, then you will see 2 parts displayed in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard. Also, in this case, you will not be able to add parallel parts or remove existing parallel parts.
- If the multiplicity is defined as an interval (for example, 0..*, 1..10, 1..* ) or *, only one part will be created by default. If you need more parts, you can create them by pressing **Insert** or selecting **Add parallel part** from the selected property’s shortcut menu.
- If the multiplicity is 1, parts will not be created.

The parts and properties are displayed with the suggested types, names, and values. In this step of the wizard, you can change them. You can also change the type for the part only if there is an available sub-element for that part in your model (see the Case study).

**Selecting a value for an instance**

To select a value for an instance

1. Select a part to be instantiated to which you want to add a value.
2. In **Instantiated properties**, click a **Value** specification cell and then click the ... button.
3. The **Select Instance Specification** dialog opens.
4. In the opened dialog, select the instance specification you want to add for the selected instance.
5. Click the **OK** button.

To create a value for an instance

1. Select a part to be instantiated for which you want to create a value.
2. In **Instantiated properties**, click the **Value** specification cell and then click the **Show Shortcut Menu** button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened shortcut menu, point to the **Value Specification** and then select one of the available value types.
4. In the **Value property** specification cell, type the desired value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Changing the name of an instance**

Each instance has a defaultname that uses the dot notation. However, you can change the default name by clicking the value row next to the **Name** property and typing the new name for the instance. ****

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ****

**Applying a custom stereotypeto an instance**

You can also apply the custom stereotype to the instance. Only those stereotypes can be applied that have *Instance Specification* as their metaclass and whose customizations have a meta property named *classifier*with the **New Types** property value set to the instantiated element’s type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The custom «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes have *Instance Specification* as their metaclass 
and their respective customizations *ControllerFMU Customization* and *ControllerSTM Customization* have a meta property named classifier with the **New Types** property value set to the «Controller» stereotype. 
Since the *Controller* class has the «Controller» stereotype applied, the «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes can be applied to the *Controller* class's instance.

**Changing a type for an instance**

You can change the type for the property's instance only if that type is a subtype of a Class (connected throughGeneralization). As shown in the following figure, the *Engine GM* Class and *Engine GMR C*lass are subtypes of the *Engine*Class. When creating an instance for the *Vehicle* Class, you can change the type of the *engine*Property by selecting one of the following: *Engine GM* Class, *Engine GMR* Class, or *Engine*Class.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the type for a Part Property instance directly in the Automatic Instantiation dialog.

##### Step #2. Selecting a package

In this step, you can select the package to store instance specifications. You can select anexisting package from the element tree, create a new package, or clone selectedpackages. Click the **Finish** buttonafter this step if you do not want to display your created instance specifications.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a new Package in the element tree

1. In the element tree, select the Package for which you want to create the new one.
2. Click the **Creation Mode** button.
3. Click the **Create Owner** button.
4. From the open menu, select the package kind. The Specification window of that package opens.
5. Type its name.
6. Click **Close**.

To clone the Packages in the element tree

1. In the element tree, select the Package to clone. The Package is cloned recursively including all nested structures.
2. Click the **Clone** button.
3. The Specification window of cloned Package opens.
4. Type its name.
5. Click **Close**.

##### Step #3. Creating a diagram (optional)

In this step, you can choose whether you want to show your created instance specifications in a new diagram or not. If you do not wish to create a new diagram, you can simply skip this step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a diagram using **Automatic Instantiation** Wizard

1. If you choose to create a new diagram for displaying instance specification(s), select the **Create a new diagram**check box.
2. If you wish to create and display links between your instance specification(s), select the **Create link between instances**check box.
3. Fill in the **Type diagram name**field by typing the diagram name. NoteBy default, the diagram name is the selected Package's name in Step 2.
4. From the **Select diagram type** drop-down list, you can select one of the diagram types.
5. Specify the owner for a new diagram:
  - Select existing Packages from the tree.
  - Click the **Create Owner** button to create a new one.
  - Click the **Clone** button to clone existing Packages.
6. After clicking the **Finish** button, a new diagram with instance specifications is created and displayed in your project.

907308985

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML v1' space='']Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML Plugin

</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To select a value for <span>an instance</span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select a part to be instantiated to which you want to add a value. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In <strong>Instantiated properties</strong>, click a <strong>Value</strong> specification cell and then click the ... button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <strong>Select Instance Specification</strong> dialog opens.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the opened dialog, select the instance specification you want to add for the selected instance.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>OK</strong> button. </span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /> </span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a value for <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">an instance</span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select a part to be instantiated for which you want to create a value.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In <strong>Instantiated properties</strong>, click the <strong>Value</strong> specification cell and then click the <strong>Show Shortcut Menu</strong> button. </span> <br /> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_shortcut_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the opened shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Value Specification</strong> and then select one of the available value types.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Value property</strong> specification cell, type the desired value.</span> <br /> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="value_property_with_defined_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /> </span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Changing the name of an instance</span> </strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Each instance has a <span>default </span>name that uses the dot notation. However, you can change the default name by clicking the value row next to the <strong>Name</strong> property and typing the new name for the instance. </span> <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span> </strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Changing_the_name_of_a_part.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <br /> </span> </span> </strong></p><p><strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span>Applying a custom</span> <span>stereotype </span><span>to an instance</span> </span> </strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can also apply the custom stereotype to the instance. Only those stereotypes can be applied that have </span> <em style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Instance Specification</em> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">as their metaclass and whose customizations have a meta property named <em>classifier </em></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">with the </span> <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">New Types</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">property value set to the instantiated element’s type.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Apply_a_custom_stereotype_to_instance_UML.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;">The custom «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes have <em>Instance Specification</em> as their metaclass <br />and their respective customizations <em>ControllerFMU Customization</em> and <em>ControllerSTM Customization</em> have a meta property named classifier with the <strong>New Types</strong> property value set to the «Controller» stereotype. <br />Since the <em>Controller</em> class has the «Controller» stereotype applied, the «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes can be applied to the <em>Controller</em> class's instance.</h6><p><strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Changing a type for an instance</span> </strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can change the type for the property's instance only if that type is a subtype of a Class (connected <span>through </span>Generalization). As shown in the following figure, the <em>Engine GM</em> Class and <em>Engine GMR C</em>lass are subtypes of the <em>Engine </em>Class. When creating an instance for the <em>Vehicle</em> Class, you can change the type of the <em>engine </em>Property by selecting one of the following: <em>Engine GM</em> <span> Class, <em>Engine GMR</em> </span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Class</span>, or <em>Engine </em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Class</span></span><span>.</span> </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_type_for_instantiated_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Changing the type for a Part Property instance directly in the Automatic Instantiation dialog.</span></h6><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Step #2. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Selecting a package</span> </span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In this step, you can select the package to store instance specifications. You can select an </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">existing package from the element tree, create a new package, or clone selected </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">packages.</span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">after this step if you do not want to display your created instance specifications.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_package_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a new Package in the element tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the element tree, select the Package for which you want to create the new one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> button. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>Create Owner</strong> button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the open menu, select the package kind. The Specification window of that package opens.<br /> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Type its name.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To clone the Packages <span>in the element tree</span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the element tree, select the Package to clone.<br /> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eeb6f4cf-5cbe-45ab-9321-bf829e0ba307"><ac:rich-text-body>The Package is cloned recursively including all nested structures.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>Clone</strong> button.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Specification window of cloned Package opens.<br /> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Type its name.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Step #3. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Creating a diagram (optional)</span> </span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In this step, you can choose whether you want to show your created instance specifications in a new diagram or not. If you do not wish to create a new diagram, you can simply skip this step.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_diagram_step3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a diagram using <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> Wizard</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If you choose to create a new diagram for displaying instance specification(s), select the <strong>Create a new diagram </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">check box</span>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If you wish to create and display links between your instance specification(s), <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">select the </span> <strong>Create link between instances </strong>check box.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Fill in the </span> <strong>Type diagram name </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">field by typing the diagram name.<br /> </span> </span> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4db12659-8cdc-4979-b226-9ee2bb0450af"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> By default, the diagram name is the selected Package's name in Step 2.<br /> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the <strong>Select diagram type</strong> drop-down list, you can select one of the diagram types.</span></li><li>Specify the owner for a new diagram:<ul><li>Select existing Packages from the tree.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span>Click the <strong>Create Owner </strong></span></span>button to create a new one.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the <strong>Clone</strong> button to clone existing Packages.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After clicking the <strong>Finish</strong> button, a new diagram with instance specifications is created and displayed in your project. </span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efee3e9e-7b4f-43e7-8821-e2cdc94040d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907308985</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study" /></ac:link></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML v1" /><ac:link-body><span class="current">Automatic Instantiation wizard</span> in SysML Plugin</ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02093: (2026x Refresh1) Working with C1 Capability Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `304000567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000567/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+C1+Capability+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:25.043+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)** provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.

To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Capability Taxonomy Table (C1)</strong> provides all the capabilities referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, it can be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Capability in the Capability Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose the desired Capabilities.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Kind, Definition, and URI for the Capability.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990412 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02094: (2026x Refresh1) Working with cells

- page_id: `303990412`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990412/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+cells
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:27.673+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Data types

1343270176

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Data types

1343270178

1343270175

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Data types

1343270177

This section explains how to work with cells in the table: selection, entering and clearing values, opening an element's Specification window, and navigation.

##### selectingSelecting cells

In all tables, you can select:

- 
- Multiple cells.
- Entire table.

singleTo select a single cell

1. Press the left mouse button on the table.

multipleTo select multiple cells, do one of the following

- Press the left mouse button and drag a mouse pointer.
- Press Shift and use the left mouse button or any of the arrow keys from the keyboard.

entireTo select an entire table

1. Right-click the column # header.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Select command. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_table.png']

##### Entering values into a cell

Individual cells in the table cannot be edited if the text is highlighted in grey. This means the cell contains a predefined value and is non-editable.

All changes made in the table are saved in the model. The procedure below describes how to enter a value into an editable cell.

To enter a value into a cell

1. Double-click a cell you want to edit.
2. One of the following actions is available, depending on the property type of the column in which the selected cell is found: - Type the value directly in the selected cell. - Click the Edit button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing property values' space=''] opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='gt_edit_cell.png'] - Click the Add (+) to specify a value. - Select the value from the drop down list.
3. In the opened property value editor specify required properties. The value is saved in the model.

##### Opening the Specification window of an element from a cell

The property can be edited in the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] if it is not locked.

To open the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] from a cell

Do either:

- If the cell is non-editable, double-click it.
- If the cell is editable, right-click on a cell, and from the shortcut menu select Specification. Then: [ATTACHMENT filename='gt_open_specification_submenu.png']
  - If the selected cell does not refer to any element, the Specification window of the element in the row will open.
  - If the selected cell refers to more than one element, the submenu with the list of appropriate elements will open. Choose the element whose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] you want to open.

##### Clearing values from cells

You can clear values from a single or multiple cells in cells containing the following data types: String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration. You can clear the whole table, column, or row.

#### WARNING: Data types

Data types

Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.

The procedures below describe how to clear values from individual cells, from a block of cells, or from the entire table:

- 
- 

clear from cellsTo clear values from cells

1. Select single or multiple cells in the table. ]]>
2. Do one of the following: Selected cells of String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types become empty. The following example demonstrates that the Documentation cell is empty after clearing because its data type is a String. The Is Abstract cell value also is cleared because the value type is Boolean. [ATTACHMENT filename='cleared_cells.png']
  - From the shortcut menu, select the Clear command (see the following figure).
  - Press Delete from keyboard (see the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='clear_cell.png']

clear from tableTo clear values from an entire table

1. Select the entire table. ]]>
2. Do one of the following: - From the table shortcut menu, click the Clear command. - Press Delete on the keyboard.

##### Navigation between cells

Navigate between cells using the arrow keys from the keyboard and keyboard shortcuts.

| Press | To move... |
| --- | --- |
| Right Arrow key | One cell to the right. |
| Left Arrow key | One cell to the left. |
| Down Arrow key | One cell down. |
| Up Arrow key | One cell up. |
| Tab | One cell to the right. |
| Shift+Tab | To the previous cell. |
| Enter | To the cell below. |
| Shift+Enter | To the cell above. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8d39acf-06a8-4f1e-9eaa-b7a2b3db5e73"><ac:parameter The procedure below describes how to enter a value into an editable cell.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enter a value into a cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a cell you want to edit.</li><li>One of the following actions is available, depending on the property type of the column in which the selected cell is found:<br />- Type the value directly in the selected cell.<br />- Click the <strong>Edit</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Editing property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[property value editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_edit_cell.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with cells" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />- Click the <strong>Add</strong> (+) to specify a value.<br />- Select the value from the drop down list.</li><li>In the opened property value editor specify required properties.<br />The value is saved in the model.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><br /></p><h3>Opening the Specification window of an element from a cell</h3><p>The property can be edited in the element’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> if it is not locked.</p><p>To open the element’s <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> from a cell</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ul><li>If the cell is non-editable, double-click it.</li><li>If the cell is editable, right-click on a cell, and from the shortcut menu select Specification. Then:<br /><ul><li>If the selected cell does not refer to any element, the Specification window of the element in the row will open.</li><li>If the selected cell refers to more than one element, the submenu with the list of appropriate elements will open. Choose the element whose <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> you want to open.</li></ul><ac:image ac:title="Submenu of Open Specification command" ac:alt="Submenu of Open Specification command"><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_open_specification_submenu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with cells" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><h3><span>Clearing values from cells</span></h3><p>You can clear values from a single or multiple cells in cells containing the following data types: String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration. You can clear the whole table, column, or row.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2971b0bd-dbbf-4154-8b9f-be21f40ee826"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Data types</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The procedures below describe how to clear values from individual cells, from a block of cells, or from the entire table:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="clear from cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to clear values from cells]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="clear from table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to values from the entire table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a50da066-96e1-45af-9dff-8f75d3e8b2c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">clear from cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To clear values from cells</p><hr /><ol><li>Select single or multiple cells in the table. <ac:link ac:anchor="selecting"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to select cells in the table >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Clear</strong> command (see the following figure).</li><li>Press <strong>Delete</strong> from keyboard (see the following figure).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clear_cell.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with cells" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul>Selected cells of String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types become empty. The following example demonstrates that the <em>Documentation</em> cell is empty after clearing because its data type is a String. The <em>Is Abstract</em> cell value also is cleared because the value type is Boolean.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cleared_cells.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with cells" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fe9a746-e02d-4fbb-a87e-0ae3e0ac5122"><ac:parameter ac:name="">clear from table</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To clear values from an entire table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the entire table. <ac:link ac:anchor="selecting"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to select cells in table >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - From the table shortcut menu, click the <strong>Clear</strong> command.<br /> - Press Delete on the keyboard.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span><br /></span></p><h3><span>Navigation between cells</span></h3><p>Navigate between cells using the arrow keys from the keyboard and keyboard shortcuts.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Press</th><th>To move...</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Right Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell to the right.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Left Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell to the left.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Down Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell down.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Up Arrow key</td><td colspan="1">One cell up.</td></tr><tr><td>Tab</td><td>One cell to the right.</td></tr><tr><td>Shift+Tab</td><td>To the previous cell.</td></tr><tr><td>Enter</td><td>To the cell below.</td></tr><tr><td>Shift+Enter</td><td>To the cell above.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990175 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02095: (2026x Refresh1) Working with columns

- page_id: `303990175`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990175/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+columns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:16.426+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Additional information

1344888677

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Additional information

1344888679

1344888676

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Additional information

1344888678

This section shows how to work with columns in the table: selection, clearing values, hiding, reordering, adding, and removing columns.

##### Selecting an entire column

To select an entire column

1. Right-click the column header.
2. From the shortcut menu, click the Select command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Clearing values from an entire column

#### WARNING: Data types

Data types

Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.

To clear values from an entire column

1. Right-click the column header.
2. Do one of the following: - From the shortcut menu, click the Clear command. - Press Delete from the keyboard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding a column

To hide a column

1. Select single or multiple cells, and from the shortcut menu, select Column > Hide .
2. Right-click the column header to open the shortcut menu, and select the Hide command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Reordering columns

To reorder columns

- Click the column header and drag it to the desired place. Only the first column cannot be moved.

##### Adding and removing columns

To add or remove columns

1. To open the available columns list: Using the **Select Columns** dialog, you can choose to display the properties of the specified element type, as well as the properties that are subtypes of the specified element type.
  - On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Table toolbars' space=''] , click the Columns to open the menu with common properties corresponding to element types that have been selected for the table.
  - On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Table toolbars' space=''] , click Columns > Select Columns to open the dialog with all properties, including tags of stereotypes corresponding to selected element types.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the properties you want to see as columns in the table.
  - Unselect the properties you do not want to see as columns in the table.

##### Adding and removing Custom Columns

Custom Columns allow you to create structured expressions the results of which are only visible in the table for which the Custom Column is created. If you prefer to create an expression that can be reusable in multiple tables, matrices, maps, and other locations where structured expressions are accepted, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Custom traceability rules' space=''] instead.

To add Custom Columns

1. In the toolbar, click **Show Columns**/**Columns** > **New Custom Column**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the **Expression** dialog, specify criteria for the Custom Column, and click **OK**.

To remove Custom Columns

1. Right-click the Custom Column header you want to remove.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Remove Custom Column command. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_custom_column.png']

##### Specifying Custom Columns

When you create a new Custom Column, you need to define the expression in the **Expression** dialog. Keep in mind:

- The Custom Column type and multiplicity (conveyed via the Single Value check box) are set automatically according to the specified expression once it is created or edited. You can also change them manually, however, they will only be applied if you make no modifications to the expression itself. [ATTACHMENT filename='derived_property_type_and_multiplicity_img.png']
- The root union of a Custom Column structured expression has the Unique check box which you can select to have only one identical element count as the result displayed in the table column cell. By default, the check box is selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='unique_ordered_options.png']

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

- To learn more about specifying expressions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] page.
- You can also select to show implied relationships in the table. The procedure for specifying which relationships to display in the table is the same as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting criteria' space=''] .

##### Editing Custom Columns

To edit a Custom Column

1. In a table, right-click the header of the desired Custom Column and select Edit Custom Column .
2. In the Expression dialog, make desired changes and click OK .

##### Setting detailed column names

Column names in the table header are set automatically. They are element property names. You cannot change column names. If an element has some properties with the same name (for example, in associations or if a tag of stereotypes is selected as a column), detailed column names could provide more precise information.

To see detailed column names

- On the Options toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] and then select Show Detailed Column Name . The group name (between brackets) to which the selected property belongs, and/or the stereotype name (just before the property name), will be displayed in the column heading.

The following diagram shows three different columns with the same name for the association element: **Name**, **Name** (Role of A), and **Name** (Role of B), and a column for the class element with the stereotype «Teacher». **Name** (Tags). If the command **Show Detailed Column Name** were not selected, there would be four columns with the same headings, i.e., **Name**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000389 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02096: (2026x Refresh1) Working with conjugated Interfaces

- page_id: `304000389`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000389/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+conjugated+Interfaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:06.228+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The <viewpoint name>*Conjugated Interface (~Interface) is a specialization ofInterface that has the same features as its original Interface except that itsFlow Propertiesare reversed (conjugated).

To create a Conjugated <viewpoint name>* Interface

- Right-click one or more interfaces and select Tools > Create Conjugated <viewpoint name>*Interface . The Conjugated <viewpoint name>* Interface is created with the same Flow Properties as the original but with opposite directions.

As shown in the figure below, the *out**/in*Resource Interface is conjugated to ~in*/out* which has the same*test results and date*Flow Properties but in opposite directions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

*******<viewpoint name>** - Operational, Resource, Service, Resource Service

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Conjugated Interface (~Interface) is a specialization of<span> </span></span>Interface <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">that has the same features as its original Interface except that its</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span></span>Flow Properties<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>are reversed (conjugated). </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;*</span> Interface</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click one or more interfaces and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Interface</strong>.<br />The<span> </span>Conjugated <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">&lt;viewpoint name&gt;* </span>Interface is created with the same Flow Properties as the original but with opposite directions. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">As shown in the figure below, the <em>out</em></span><em>/in</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> Resource Interface is conjugated to ~in<em>/out</em> which has the same </span><em>test results and date </em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Flow Properties but in opposite directions. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="conjugated_interface_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with conjugated Interfaces" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><hr /><p><strong>* </strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>&lt;viewpoint name&gt;</strong> - Operational, Resource, Service, Resource Service</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991406 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02097: (2026x Refresh1) Working with CV-5 typical table

- page_id: `303991406`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991406/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+CV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:42.157+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Capability viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

627926007

627926020

627931615

**On this page**

627926010

##### Creating CV-5 typical table

The context of the CV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating CV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the CV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the CV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the CV-5 typical table table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a CV-5 typical table

To create an empty CV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more CV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a CV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a CV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627926006

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae3d9710-8f3c-41dd-9fe0-ea64577c220f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926007</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02b19f1f-9244-4fa5-a6d6-32e0d9f05038"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1ef6059-4e8e-4453-afcc-2147b9ab361d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627931615</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c81faddc-31ed-4f8d-923a-5cf808482dfd" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bb78b52-7bea-4f5a-82ca-0734b04f8899"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926010</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating CV-5 typical table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the CV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating CV-1 diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with CV-5 typical table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The content of the CV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the CV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.</p><p>Resources are related to Capabilities using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the table cell is filled.</p><p>Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.</span> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-2-11_14-17-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with CV-5 typical table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.</p><p>There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the CV-5 typical table table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a CV-5 typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a CV-5 typical table</span></h4><p>To create an empty CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong> table.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><p>An<em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6cdec1b-3c94-432e-961c-856ebfd8a021"><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.</li><li>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.</li><li>versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.</li><li>Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more CV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Resources from a CV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Resources from a CV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the CV-5 Capability to Organizational Deployment Mapping (typical) table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f02f439-4c17-411c-8176-39a1534fb195"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627926006</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997106 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02098: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Date and Time Summary Table

- page_id: `303997106`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997106/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Date+and+Time+Summary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:53.210+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Summary and Overview
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1726920497

1726920518

1726920508

The **Date and Time Summary Table (Sm-Ov)** depicts all dates and times specified in the ISO8601 date-time format.

Adding the date-time elements into the table, you can see their defined values, owners, and the list of the elements in which the date-time elements are used as tagged values.

To add existing model elements

1. In the table toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table. If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.
3. Click **OK** when you are done.

The selected date-time elements are added as rows.

1726920495

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

The <strong>Select ISO8601 Date Time</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the dialog, choose which date-time elements you need to add to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b7b60e6-1344-45fe-bb0b-299147c13860"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to add more than one element, click the Multiple Selection button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><p>The selected date-time elements are added as rows.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8290fc10-3a7c-4366-8b93-3f90c517d309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1726920495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994529 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02099: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Dictionary diagram

- page_id: `303994529`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994529/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Dictionary+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:19.892+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Architecture Management > (2026x Refresh1) Architecture Management Information
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Dictionary (Am-If)** provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.

To create a new or add an existing term

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Dictionary (Am-If)</strong> provides definitions of all terms used throughout the architectural data.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new or add an existing term</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, choose the elements which represent the terms.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992516 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02100: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Enterprise Architect Import

- page_id: `303992516`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992516/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Enterprise+Architect+Import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:00.662+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Importing data from other UML tools and formats > (2026x Refresh1) Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

479168392

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

100.00002%

479168395

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

479168394

Enterprise Architect Import (EA) will automatically load when you start a program. You can use it to import UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 to your modeling tool.

To import Enterprise Architect UML XMI file

1. On the main menu of the modeling tool, click File > Import From > Enterprise Architect UML2.1 XMI 2.1 File . The Import Enterprise Architect Files dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Import Enterprise Architect Files.png']
2. In the dialog, you must specify:
  - The input file - the XMI file exported from Enterprise Architecture NoteEnterprise Architect Import supports the EA XMI files exported with the option specified as: XMI Type = **UML2.1(XMI 2.1).**
  - The output file name
  - A conversion option
3. Click Import to import the file.

The following table describes conversion options:

| Conversion Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Switch aggregation side | To configure the aggregation switch-side. This option is recommended for XMI files exported from EA 7.1. |
| Exclude EA specific data | To exclude all EA-specific data from being imported (see Transforming EA Specific Data to see a list of EA specific-data that can be transformed into UML elements with stereotypes). |
| Open project after conversion | To load the output project file once the conversion process has been completed. |
| Generate conversion log | To generate a conversion log and save it in the same folder as the output file. The same conversion information will also be displayed on the Notification Window.NoteYou can open the window by pressing Ctrl + M. |

479168391

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2c395ef-30e4-4a9e-890c-5671855aa562"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">479168392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7c77641-ca09-44b0-b9f7-b9eb9b15e8d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">100.00002%</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="id">479168395</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="196ef441-324d-45c5-811f-78c4946023c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">479168394</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enterprise Architect Import (EA) will automatically load when you start a program. You can use it to import UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 to your modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import Enterprise Architect UML XMI file</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu of the modeling tool, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Enterprise Architect UML2.1 XMI 2.1 File</strong>. The <strong>Import Enterprise Architect Files </strong>dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Import Enterprise Architect Files.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Enterprise Architect Import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the dialog, you must specify:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The input file - the XMI file exported from Enterprise Architecture</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d9348616-32c6-4af8-8b8f-e75b60069ad0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enterprise Architect Import supports the EA XMI files exported with the option specified as: XMI Type = <strong>UML2.1(XMI 2.1).</strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>The output file name</li><li>A conversion option</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Import </strong>to import the file.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The following table describes conversion options:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 306.0px;" /><col style="width: 721.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Conversion Option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><span>Switch aggregation side</span></td><td><span>T</span><span>o<span> </span>configure the <strong>aggregation<span> </span>switch-side</strong>.<span> </span>This option is recommended for<span> </span>XMI<span> </span>files exported from EA<span> </span>7.1.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Exclude EA<span> </span>specific data</span></td><td><span>T</span><span>o<span> </span>exclude all EA-specific data from being imported (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Transforming EA Specific Data" /><ac:link-body><span>T</span>ransforming<span> </span>EA<span> </span>Specific Data</ac:link-body></ac:link> to<span> </span>see a list of EA<span> </span>specific-data that<span> </span>can be transformed into UML elements with stereotypes).</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Open project after<span> </span>conversion</span></td><td><span>T</span><span>o<span> </span>load the output project file once the conversion process has been completed.</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Generate conversion log</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To generate a conversion log and save it in the same folder as the output file. The same conversion information will also be displayed on the <strong>Notification Window</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5855b8a2-1ee4-44bf-9368-c49e1f5d0f9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can open the window by pressing <strong>Ctrl </strong>+ <strong>M</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8ef30db-90de-454c-b8ee-1581eff1de08"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">479168391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d7a176a3-ea9a-4b8b-a9c6-99e9d68ceaba" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999223 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02101: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Environment diagram

- page_id: `303999223`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999223/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Environment+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:41.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Parameters Pm
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment** diagram shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.

The Environment diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)

##### Creating a diagram

An example is shown with the Operational Parameters, but the Environment diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.

To create an Environment diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Parameters and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Environment**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Environment**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Location element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Location in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Location and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Location in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Operational Structure (or Taxonomy, Connectivity, Constraints) package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Location and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_location.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Location

When you have the Location created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Condition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Environment' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Location' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Property Set' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resource' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Asset' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Condition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Property' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Geo Political Extent Type' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Location' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Location Holder' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Performer' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
