- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Parameters Pm
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram (Mi-Pm-VS)** shows the elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments and conditions applicable to capability, operational concept, or set of systems.

The Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g, Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)

##### Creating a diagram

An example is shown with the Strategic Parameters, but the Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.

To create a Mission Vignette and Scenario diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Parameters and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Mission Vignette and Scenario**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for****Mission** Vignette and Scenario,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Mission Scenario element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Mission Scenario in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Mission Scenario and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Mission Scenario in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Strategic Parameters package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Mission Scenario and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_mission_scenatio.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Mission Scenario (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Mission Scenario

When you have the Mission Scenario elements created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

## PAGE 02116: (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements

- page_id: `303994714`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994714/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:42.049+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Containment Tree

29726528

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Containment Tree

29726530

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Containment Tree

29726529

#### EXCERPT: Containment Tree

INLINE

The chapter outlines various methods of working with your model's elements. You will find detailed instructions on creating and defining model elements, default property values, extracting, defining hyperlinks, etc. You will also learn how the modeling tool shortcut menus, toolbar, and browser enable you to easily edit model elements.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']true

To create elements in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment Tree, right-click the element owner where you want to create a new model element.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Create Element .
3. A list with available elements appears. Select the element you need or start typing an element name in the Search box to find it faster.
4. Type the name of the element.

You can also create an element directly on the diagram pane. In this case, a symbol of the element and the element in the diagram owner is created.

To create elements on the diagram pane

1. Open the diagram or create a new diagram where you want to create a new model element.
2. From the diagram palette, select an element and drag it on the diagram pane. The element symbol is created.
3. Click the symbol and type the element name.

#### TIP: Containment Tree

Containment Tree

Please note, the new element is created in the Model Browser as well.

29726527

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993786 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02118: (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements in the Model Browser

- page_id: `303993786`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993786/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+model+elements+in+the+Model+Browser
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:57.588+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface > (2026x Refresh1) Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1426024704

1426024708

1426024707

INLINE

In the Model Browser, you can do the various actions with model elements, such as rename, cut, copy, delete, and others. These actions are available from the particular model element shortcut menu. See the list of concrete actions in the following table.

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Rename | Switches the element name into the edit mode. You can also press F2 for this. |
| Copy | Copies the selected element. |
| Copy URL | Copies a model element URL to a clipboard. |
| Copy Element Hyperlink | Copies the hyperlink of the selected element. |
| Cut | Cuts the selected element. |
| Paste | Pastes the copied element. |
| Delete | Deletes the selected element. |

To copy/cut and paste the selected model element between different projects

1. Select the model element in the Model Browser. 
 2. From the model element shortcut menu, select **Copy** or **Cut**. 
 3. Open the project to which you want to paste the selected element(s). 
 4. Select the container, where you wish to paste the element(s). 
 5. From the container's shortcut menu, select **Paste**.

To change the location of an element in the Model Browser

1. Select the element in the Model Browser. 
 2. Drag it to the new location (possible owner).

1426024703

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

To start the**Model Transformation Wizard**

- From the Tools menu, choose Model Transformations .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991870 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02120: (2026x Refresh1) Working with multiple projects

- page_id: `303991870`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991870/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+multiple+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:18.416+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Creating projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428492948

1428492951

1428492950

INLINE

You may need to manage several projects at the same time, and the modeling tool allows you to do that easily. All open projects are held in separate workspaces. Different active projects may exchange data. Entities from one project can be copied or moved to another.

To switch between open projects, do one of the following

- From File menu, select Switch Projects and then click the name of the project you want to switch to.
- On the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_projects_button.png'] and then select the name of the project you want to switch to. [ATTACHMENT filename='switching_between_projects.png']

To close all open projects

- Select Close All Projects from the File menu. The Question message appears.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Choose the way your projects will be closed:

| Yes | The project you are currently closing will be saved (its name appears in the question). The dialog is displayed again when the next project closes. |
| --- | --- |
| Yes To All | Save all projects without prompting. The Save dialog will not appear for each open project. |
| No | Project you are currently closing will not be saved. The dialog is displayed again when the next project closes. |
| No To All | All the projects will be closed without saving or further prompting. |
| Cancel | Cancel saving projects. |

To exchange model elements between open projects

- Use the Cut , Copy , and Paste commands from the Edit menu or the selected element's shortcut menu, or the appropriate shortcut keys: Ctrl + X, Ctrl + C, Ctrl + V.

1428492947

**Rrelated pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project Properties dialog' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000560 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02121: (2026x Refresh1) Working with NCV-5 typical table

- page_id: `304000560`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000560/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+NCV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:24.812+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

628010590

628010604

628010588

**On this page**

628010594

##### Creating NCV-5 typical table

The context of the NCV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating NCV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the NCV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the NCV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the NCV-5 typical table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a NCV-5 typical table

To create an empty NCV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a NCV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more NCV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 typical table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

628015084

ac:macro-id="f6cdec1b-3c94-432e-961c-856ebfd8a021"><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.</li><li>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.</li><li>versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.</li><li>Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more NCV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Resources from a NCV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Resources from a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 typical table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cd85468-a942-4243-ae40-7864a6e589ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628015084</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990846 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02122: (2026x Refresh1) Working with NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `303990846`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990846/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+NSOV-3+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:04.143+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **NSOV-3****Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>NSOV-3 </strong><strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994218 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02123: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `303994218`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994218/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Activities+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:06.939+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282484386

1282484407

1282484397

The Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Activities and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create an Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps To Capability relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Activity to a Capability

To map an Operational Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282484385

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994808 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02124: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `303994808`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994808/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:48.960+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Operational Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Operational Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Operational Connectivity and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

An Operational Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges

Before creating Operational Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Operational Information, Resource Information, or Resource). The you can start creating Operational Exchanges.

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Operational Performer and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Operational Exchange . Click another Operational Performer to connect. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Operational Exchange** and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

##### Realizing Operational Exchanges on Associations

When you have Operational Performers connected with an Association, there are two ways to create operational exchanges:

- Opening Exchange Manager.
- Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.

###### Opening the Exchange Manager

To create new Operational Exchange on the Association

1. Click the Association between Operational Performers.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Operational Exchange. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_op_exchange.png']
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
5. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog.

To open an **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.
2. In the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Operational Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Operational Exchanges on the Association.

###### Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association

You can create the Operational Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the wizard, specify the needed information.

In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="707fdfcb-d53e-4825-bd53-df7c62c87aa2" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p>To create an Operational Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Connectivity and do one of the following:</span><ul><li>From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Operational Connectivity</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Operational Connectivity </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_op_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1810a3e-531e-49fa-b3c9-57d324bb8ed3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. (<em>Optional</em>)</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_exchange_on_association.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open an <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">an Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li>In the smart manipulator, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange Manager"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange_manager.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.<br />The <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_exchange_manager.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Operational Exchanges on the Association.</p><h4>Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association</h4><p>You can create the Operational Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_1_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In the wizard, specify the needed information. </p><p>In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994840 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02125: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Connectivity Table

- page_id: `303994840`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994840/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:49.691+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An **Operational Connectivity Table** addresses operational exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add new or existing Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Operational Exchanges

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An <strong>Operational Connectivity Table</strong> addresses operational exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995131 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02126: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `303995131`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995131/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:11.912+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Operational Performers.
2. Connect them using the Associations relationship.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.

An Operational Constraints Definition view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] diagram.

##### Creating an Operational Exchange in Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of Operational Performers and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994858 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02127: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Constraints table

- page_id: `303994858`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994858/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:50.384+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] and in the Select Subject Of Operational Constraint dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Operational Information, Operational Exchange).
3. In the **Rule** **Specification**cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the **Rule Kind** from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Operational Constraint for one constraint.

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In Select Operational Constraint dialog, select Operational Constraints. The filled row is added in table.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995018 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02128: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `303995018`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995018/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+High+Level+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:00.111+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="wiki-content"><p>An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p>To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol></div>
````

1. Create Operational Information .
2. Create Association relationships between Operational Information .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994792 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02130: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `303994792`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994792/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:48.685+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

3

##### Creating Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

The owner of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram must be an Operational Performer.

To create an Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Operational Roles.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

Create the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Composite Structure element using any method that is most suitable for you:

- Right-click the Composite element in a Structure Diagram and Create Diagram.
- Right-click the Composite element in the Containment Tree and Create Diagram.
- Select the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Smart Manipulator.

The diagram creation wizard is displayed, showing the hierarchical list of part properties owned by the selected composite element. All the elements or any subset can be included in the diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges in Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector between a pair of Operational Roles, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector .
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Operational Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Operational Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connector </span>between a pair of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational </span>Roles, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click<span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Operational Exchange" ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Operational Exchange" ac:alt="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connector</span>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Operational Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.</li><li>Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Operational Exchange on the diagram pane. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish </strong>when you are done. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_conveyed_item_palette.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4c4befc-85e4-439e-84bb-0c6c8f57299d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="903c6687-2972-4b79-b1c3-1e79f1f46b51"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Learn more</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ac:link>.</strong> You can</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> check the consistency of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 02131: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Parametric diagram

- page_id: `303995144`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995144/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:13.960+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Parameters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

495805059

495805061

495805060

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED']to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED']and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

495805058

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994241 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02132: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `303994241`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994241/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Performers+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:07.244+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****displays the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational performers that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Performers, Operational Architecturesand their specializations, andthe Columns areCapabilities.

To create the Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Performers, Operational Architectures).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] Refresh button.

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Performer to a Capability

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map Operational Performers to a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with matrices' space='']

Architectures).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994952 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02133: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Process Flow diagram

- page_id: `303994952`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994952/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Process+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:56.147+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Processes diagram' space=''] must be created first.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Performer or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Operational Process Flow diagram with the top element -*Distress Signal Handling*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Operational Activity Action or Operational Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Operational Process Flow diagram for an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Operational Activity Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Operational Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Operational Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Performer and Operational Activity (the behavior of the Operational Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Swimlanes" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995135 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02134: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Processes diagram

- page_id: `303995135`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995135/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:12.449+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994827 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02135: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `303994827`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994827/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Role-based+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:49.333+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add anexisting Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

1. In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click **+** button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add <span class="confluence-link">an </span><span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click <strong>+</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994942 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02136: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Sequences diagram

- page_id: `303994942`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994942/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Operational+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:55.451+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Operational > (2026x Refresh1) Operational Sequences
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Operational Roles and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram palette, select the Operational Message.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Sequences diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Operational Roles and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click</span> <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Operational Exchange" ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Sequences diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Operational Exchange" ac:alt="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Operational Sequences diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span> and on the diagram palette, select the Operational Message.</span></li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
