## PAGE 02198: (2026x Refresh1) Working with S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `304000775`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000775/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+S1+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:42.681+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Service viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **S1****Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993437 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02199: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Connectivity (Operational) Table

- page_id: `303993437`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993437/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Connectivity+Operational+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:09.452+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A **Security Connectivity (Operational) Table** represents exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add new or existing Operational Exchanges to fill in a Security Connectivity (Operational) Table.

##### Adding new Operational Exchanges

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select needed Operational Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities

1. In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Activities button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Activities dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A <strong>Security Connectivity (Operational) Table</strong> represents exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges to fill in a Security Connectivity (Operational) Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select needed Operational Exchanges.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other<span> cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span></span><span> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target" style="">Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="">To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li>In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Activities</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the open<span> </span><strong>Producing and Consuming Activities<span> </span></strong>dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997113 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02200: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Connectivity (Resource) Table

- page_id: `303997113`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997113/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Connectivity+Resource+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:53.350+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Security Connectivity (Resource) Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add new or existing Resource Exchange to fill in a Security Connectivity (Resource) Table.

##### Adding new Resource Exchanges

To add new Resource Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity (Resource) Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select needed Resource Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions

1. In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Functions dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Security Connectivity (Resource) Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchange to fill in a Security Connectivity (Resource) Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Security Connectivity (Resource) Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select needed Resource Exchanges.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other<span> cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></span><span> </span><br /></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.</li><li>In the open<span> </span><strong>Producing and Consuming Functions</strong><span> </span>dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997109 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02201: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `303997109`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997109/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:53.275+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Resources Connectivity diagram

The Security Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be completed.

To create a Security Connectivity diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The Security Connectivity product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the Security Structure diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Security Processes' space=''] as soon as the Resources Connectivity has been completed.

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Security Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in Security Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Organizational Resources.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993418 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 02202: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `303993418`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993418/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:26.077+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1163362754

1163362771

1163362753

**On this page**

1163362755

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Security Constraints Definition diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Constraints package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Constraints Definition . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_security_constraints_definition.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Constraints Definition and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements (assets). An example is described using the Security Enclave element, but the same is valid for other assets.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_security_enclave.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Security Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Security Constraints Definition diagram

To create a Resource Exchange

1. Open the **Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the asset (e.g. Security Enclave) and from its smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click another asset to connect them.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange_palette.png'] and on the diagram palette, and connect the assets.
2. Specify the Exchange Name, Resource Exchange Type.
3. Select the Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Geo Political Extent Exchange
  - Resource Communication
  - Resource Energy Flow
  - Resource Movement
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Creating and applying Security Constraints

You can create the Security Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See . Also, you can create constraints in the selected asset's Specification window.

To create a Security Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed asset and open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Security > Security Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

There are the following constrained elements that can be a subject of the Security Constraint:

1. Asset
2. Asset Role
3. Exchange
4. Security Process

To apply Security Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Security Constraint and drag it on the element.
- In the needed element's Specification window, go to Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

tree. See <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating an element" />. Also, you can create constraints in the selected asset's Specification window.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">To create a Security Constraint in the Specification window</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select a needed asset and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Constraints property group, click <strong>Create </strong>&gt; <strong>Security </strong>&gt; <strong>Security Constraint</strong>.</li><li>Specify the name.</li><li><p>In the Specification cell, define the rule. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0e4674c-3d35-46c9-8132-9ab14424f243"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The specification<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> (rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">There are the following constrained elements that can be a subject of the Security Constraint:</span></p><ol><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset Role</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Exchange</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Security Process</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">To apply Security Constraints, d</span>o one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, select a Security Constraint and drag it on the element.</li><li>In the needed element's Specification window, go to <strong>Constraints </strong>property group. Click the <strong>Apply </strong>button and from the <strong>Select Element</strong> window, choose a constraint.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993447 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02203: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Constraints table

- page_id: `303993447`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993447/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:10.034+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Security Constraints** table identifies the constraints that are imposed on systems functionality due to some aspects of system design or implementation.

To create a Security Constraints table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Select Subject Of Security Constraint** dialog, choose the needed constrained element(s):
  - Asset
  - Asset Role
  - Exchange
  - Security Process
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Security Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Security Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Security Constraints table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Constraints table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and  in the <strong>Select Subject Of Security Constraint</strong> dialog, choose the needed constrained element(s):</span><br /><ul><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset Role</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Exchange</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Security Process</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<strong> Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Constraints table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify the body.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd8254de-6c02-4462-a6df-36afcf72fabc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p><p>To add an existing Security Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select Security Constraints. The filled row is added to the table. </li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the Security Constraints table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993502 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02204: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Controls diagram

- page_id: `303993502`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993502/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Controls+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:13.157+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Motivation
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Security Controls diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Motivation and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Controls . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_security_controls.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Controls and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. To illustrate the example, Security Control and Risk elements are used, but you can also choose Enhances Security Control, Security Control Family, or Enhances as well as Security Risk.

To create a Security Control in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click Security Control and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create a Security Control in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Security Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Select Security Control .
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability.

##### Connect the elements

When you have Security Control created, you can start connecting them using the Mitigates relationship.

To connect the elements with the Mitigates relationship

1. Select an element from which the Mitigates will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Mitigates. You can also select the Mitigates relationship from the diagram palette.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create an Security Controls diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Security Motivation and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Security Controls</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="367" ac:width="329"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_security_controls.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Controls diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Security Controls </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. To illustrate the example, Security Control and Risk elements are used, but you can also choose  Enhances Security Control, Security Control Family, or Enhances as well as Security Risk.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Security Control in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click Security Control and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Security Control in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right click the Security Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Select Security Control</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.<br /></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Capability.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have Security Control created, you can start connecting them using the Mitigates relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Mitigates relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Mitigates will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Mitigates.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc7b57ae-a1f8-48e0-811e-28d2602359f3"><ac:rich-text-body>You can also select the Mitigates relationship from the diagram palette.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.</span></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993492 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02205: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `303993492`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993492/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Controls+to+Risks+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:12.453+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282871885

1282871887

1282871886

The Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix****(Sc-Tr) shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks. The Rows of this matrix are Security Controls and the Columns are Risks.

To build the matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Security Controls);
2. Specify the Column Scope (Risks);
3. Click the Refresh button.

To map an Security Controls to a Risk

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Mitigate.

To remove the relation from the matrix

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282871884

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with matrices' space='']

The Rows of this matrix are Security Controls and the Columns are Risks.</p><p> </p><p>To build the matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Security Controls);</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Risks);</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p>     </p><p>To map an Security Controls to a Risk</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li><li>Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Mitigate.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation from the matrix</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60f57012-0c0d-489c-9529-e2d5d8930201"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282871884</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with matrices" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997096 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02206: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

- page_id: `303997096`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997096/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Internal+Connectivity+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:53.053+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating a Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

The Resource Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be completed.

To create a Resource Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

1. Create Operational Roles and Operational Ports.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.

##### Creating Operational Exchange in Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_create_op_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_operational_exchange.png'] and then click the Operational Connector.
2. Select the Operational Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Configuration
  - Energy
  - Geo Political Extent
  - Information
  - Materiel
  - Organizational
3. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Operational Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Operational Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997115 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02207: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram

- page_id: `303997115`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997115/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Internal+Connectivity+Resource+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:53.411+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating a Security Internal Connectivity diagram

The Resource Structure diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be completed.

To create a Resource Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Security Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in the Security Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Resource Information can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993445 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02208: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Parametric. Operational diagram

- page_id: `303993445`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993445/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Parametric.+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:09.909+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Parameters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496649791

496649793

496649792

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Mitigation. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Role or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Mitigation context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496649790

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b53a58cb-7204-4f58-9b52-b2c98c9c4f2f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496649790</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993384 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02210: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Process Flow (Operational) diagram

- page_id: `303993384`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993384/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Security+Process+Flow+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:08.151+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Security > (2026x Refresh1) Security Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Security Process Flow diagram represents flows of security processes. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Processes diagram' space=''] must be created first.

The owner of the Security Process Flow diagram is always a Security Process.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram for Security Process Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Agent or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Agent or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Security Process Actions/Operational Activity Actions or drop Security Process/Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Security Process Actions/Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the operational structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Security Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created. You can add either Security Process Action or Operational Action in the Swimlane. You can create an inner Security Process Flow diagram for a Security Process Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar in both cases.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram from a Smart Manipulator of a Security Process Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Security Process Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Security Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Agent or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have an operational structure defined.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Security Process Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Agent and the Security Process (the behavior of the Security Process Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Security Process Action.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 02211: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Security Process Flow (Resources) diagram

- page_id: `303993390`
- space_key: `MT`
