````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994402 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02228: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Roadmap diagram

- page_id: `303994402`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994402/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+Roadmap+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:12.477+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To work with a Services Roadmap diagram

truenone

##### Create a new Version Of Configuration

To create a new Version Of Configuration in the Service Roadmap diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the Service and its specializationsfor the Version Of Configuration

To define the Service and its specializationsfor the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram pane, select a Version of Configuration, and in the diagram toolbar, click **Add New** > **Service**.
  - In the diagram pane, right-click a Version Of Configuration and select **Service**.
  - In the Containment tree, select a Service and its specializationsand drag it onto the Version of Configuration. (In this case, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens in the second step.) The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Service and its specializationsfrom a Services Roadmap diagram

To remove a Service and its specializations

1. Right-click the Service and its specializations bar, and from the shortcut menu, select Remove > Service <name of the Service and its specializations you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Logging background actions' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994299 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02229: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Role-based Connectivity table

- page_id: `303994299`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994299/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+Role-based+Connectivity+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:08.376+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The Services Role-based Connectivity Table (Sv-Cn)** specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between services.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Services Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click **+** button.In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the Services Role-based Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Service Role-based Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Service Role-based Connectivity Table report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Other cells are read-only.</span></li><li>To <span>create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use</span> Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding Service Functions</h3><p>To add Service Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Services Role-based Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Service Functions</strong> button. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Producing and Consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[choose the producing and consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Generating Reports</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The Service Role-based Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:</span></p><ul><li><span>Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the <strong>Report </strong>button,</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Using a quick report option from the Containment tree</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_role_based_connectivity_table_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Role-based Connectivity table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of the Service Role-based Connectivity Table report</span></h6><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994431 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02230: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram

- page_id: `303994431`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994431/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:14.374+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create a Services Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Services Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Services Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Services Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_service_specification.png']

Service

To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or another appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Services

When Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the ServiceRoles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Directed Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.display_related.png']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Select the element and click the Compartments, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='services_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.display_related_nested.png']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying related elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Compartments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7ac8d2c-4eb8-442c-9ab6-9d8c608da9ec" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Services Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Services Structure and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Services Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="260" ac:width="320"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_services_structure_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Services Structure </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="240" ac:width="488"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_services_service_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the Services Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Service and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="505" ac:width="353"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_services_service_specification_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_service_specification.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Services Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>(or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Services</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_composition_palette.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_structure_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Directed Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="208" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8fe10c2-93e5-4905-aae2-8a0324b428b4"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Select the element and click the Compartments, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="711a7e71-ce7a-474a-a540-955126beae32"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="services.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994415 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02231: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `303994415`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994415/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:13.453+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1160452963

1160452966

1160452961

**On this page**

1160452965

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Services Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Services Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_services_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Services Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Service Specification element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_specification.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Services Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service Specification (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Service Specification (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

/></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994452 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02232: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services Taxonomy table

- page_id: `303994452`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994452/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:15.466+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Services Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button, and in the Select Service dialog, select the desired Service. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Services Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button, and in the <strong>Select Service </strong>dialog, select the desired Service. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994446 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02233: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `303994446`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994446/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:14.818+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282964251

1282964262

1282964261

The Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Sv-Tr) depicts mapping between the Capabilities and the Services that these Capabilities enable.

The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create a Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope ( Service ).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Serviceexposes Capabilities using the Exhibitsrelationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282964250

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994477 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02234: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `303994477`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994477/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:16.586+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282974676

1282974687

1282974677

The Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix (Sv-Tr) purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.

The Rows of this matrix are Servicesand the Columns are Operational Activities.

To create a Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Operational Activities);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.

To map Operational Activitiesto Services

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282974666

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993912 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02235: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `303993912`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993912/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:42.971+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Services > (2026x Refresh1) Services Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995488 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02236: (2026x Refresh1) Working with Sr Services Roadmap

- page_id: `303995488`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995488/2026x+Refresh1+Working+with+Sr+Services+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:38.658+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Service viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) Sr Service Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921716523

INLINE

921716525

INLINE

921716524

INLINE

To work with a Services Roadmap diagram

truenone

##### Create a new Version Of Configuration

To create a new Version Of Configuration in the Service Roadmap diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the Service for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram pane, select a Version of Configuration, and in the diagram toolbar, click **Add New** > **Service**.
  - In the diagram pane, right-click a Version Of Configuration and select **Service**.
  - In the Containment tree, select a Service and drag it onto the Version of Configuration. (In this case, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens in the second step.) The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Service from a Services Roadmap diagram

To remove a Service

1. Right-click the Service bar, and from the shortcut menu, select Remove > Service <name of the resource you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.
